summary: Executed backend pytest API regression and frontend Playwright UI/integration checks against public preview URL. Core tracker flows work end-to-end with one archive formatting mismatch.

minor.issue: Archive month label is returned as 'Apr-2026' instead of expected 'Apr 2026', causing strict archive expectation mismatch.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend shows archive month exactly as API value ('Apr-2026'); expected requirement uses 'Apr 2026'.

action_items: Normalize archive month label format in backend payload (e.g., convert 'Apr-2026' to 'Apr 2026') or align acceptance criteria to source sheet format.
Retest archive section after month-label normalization.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend archive normalization currently passes month label through without display-format standardization; this can create UI/acceptance inconsistencies.
Backend file /app/backend/server.py centralizes many responsibilities (sheet IO, transformation, API routes); consider modular split before file grows further.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public endpoint tested: https://jif-budget-outturn.preview.emergentagent.com. Pytest run had 1/6 failure only on archive month label strict expectation. Desktop/mobile Playwright checks passed for counts, KPI rows/statuses, methodology, branding, and responsiveness (no horizontal overflow on 390px).
rca of the issue: Reproduction: GET /api/tracker -> inspect archive[0].month_label. Actual is 'Apr-2026'. Expected in requirement was 'Apr 2026'. Likely source is archive tab value passthrough in _normalise_archive_row without display normalization. Mitigation: normalize month label in backend response layer or update requirement/tests to accept canonical source format.
